LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT T&G 2009 LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND COURT ORDER RELEASING UNCONTESTED INTERPLED FUNDS - THE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS ARGUE AS THE SOLE BASIS FOR JUDGMENT THAT ALL OF THE OTHER DEFENDANTS HAVE DEFAULTED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW AN ENTITLEMENT TO JUDGMENT BECAUSE PARAGRAPHS 8-10, 13, AND THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF OF THE COMPLAINT DISPUTE DEFENDANT T&G'S ENTITLEMENT TO THE FUNDS. THE COURT TAKES THOSE ALLEGATIONS AS TRUE WHEN REVIEWING THIS MOTION. DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS DO NOT SUFFICIENTLY RAISE THE ISSUE REGARDING THE PROPRIETY OF THIS INTERPLEADER ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF CLAIMS AN INTEREST IN THE RES. AT ANY RATE, CITY OF MORGAN HILL V. BROWN (1999) 71 CAL.APP.4TH 1114, 1122, INDICATES THAT THIS ACTION MAY PROCEED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2015 Assigned To Trial Court

Defendant And Cross-Complainants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Its First Amened Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-07-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT T&G 2009 LLC'S Motion For Leave To File Its First Amended Cross-Complaint is Denied. The motion does not comply with the requirements of California Rule of Court 3.1324. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Court Order Releasing Uncontested Interpled Funds
Off Calendar Sep-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT T&G 2009 LLC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Court Order Releasing Uncontested Interpled Funds - The motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Defendant's moving papers argue as the sole basis for judgment that all of the other defendants have defaulted. Defendant fails to show an entitlement to judgment because paragraphs 8-10, 13, and the prayer for relief of the complaint dispute defendant T&G's entitlement to the funds. The court takes those allegations as true when reviewing this motion. Defendant's moving papers do not sufficiently raise the issue regarding the propriety of this interpleader action because plaintiff claims an interest in the res. At any rate, City of Morgan Hill v. Brown (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1114, 1122, indicates that this action may proceed. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
